title: NakliDataFile
description: A `.naklidata` workbook — the versioned, JSON-on-disk description of a NakliData session (source mounts, schema assignments, notebook cells, user types). NEVER contains source row data. Authorable from scratch: an agent can hand a user a workbook and, because the human opens and runs it, it is propose-don't-execute by construction. This schema is a CONTRACT for authoring; validation happens on load via core/persistence.ts `parse()`. Mirrors the `NakliDataFile` TS interface (src/core/persistence.ts) — keep the two in sync (a test guards the required set).

version.description: Semver-ish. Current is "1.0". A file whose version is newer than the running app is rejected on load.

sources.description: Mounted sources. Only example-bundle and single-file FSA sources persist a usable `ref`; on load, sources are re-attempted and failures show a 'Reconnect needed' banner. Row data is never here.

assignments.description: Per-column semantic-type assignments. Optional — defaults to [] on load.

cells.description: Notebook cells in order. Each must carry a string `kind`. SQL/cohort/assertion cells carry `code`.

user_types.description: User-defined semantic types. Optional — defaults to [] on load.

override_rules.description: 'Always treat columns named X as type Y' rules. Optional.

ref.description: Reattach hint (bundle id / URL / handle id); null when not persistable.

name.description: The table/view name the notebook queries.

typeId.description: Taxonomy type id, or null = unknown.

cell.description: A notebook cell. `kind` is authoritative (sql, markdown, chart, pivot, map, network, temporal, distribution, cohort, assertion, input, dashboard, stats, python, r, report, embedding). SQL/cohort/assertion carry `code`.
